subject: PATIENT
heading: Procedure:__page_no__1
text: procedure: this is a average risk patient undergoing screening colonoscopy. indications, preparation and potential complications were explained to the patient, who indicated the procedure, and signed the corresponding consent forms. mac was administered by anesthesiologist. continuous used. the pulse understanding and blood pressure monitoring were used throughout the procedure. supplemental oxygen introduced was through quality rectum of oximetry good. patient was placed in left lateral decubitus position. the colonoscope was the appendiceal orifice and and preparation advanced was under direct visualization until cecum was reached the cecal sling folds were seen. printed on [encounter_date] [patient_name], [mrn], [dob]

heading: Colonoscopy Report__page_no__1
text: colonoscopy report the ileo-cecal valve were identified. the colonoscope was retroflexed within the rectum. careful visualization was performed as the instrument was withdrawn. patient tolerance to procedure was excellent. the procedure was not difficult. digital exam was normal

subject: PATIENT
heading: Findings:__page_no__2
text: findings: excavated lesions multiple non-bleeding diverticula were seen in the sigmoid colon. localized ulceration, granularity and erythema with no bleeding were noted in the ascending colon. mucosa multiple cold forceps biopsies were performed for histology. two sessile polyps of benign appearance ranging in size from 5 mm to 6 mm were found in the transverse protruding lesions colon. a single-piece polypectomy was performed using a cold snare. the polyps were completely removed. small grade 1 internal hemorrhoids were noted.

subject: PATIENT
heading: DESCRIPTION__page_no__3
text: description the first specimen is submitted in a container of formalin labeled biopsy ascending colon colitis. it consists of tannish tissue measuring 5 x 5mm. the specimen is submitted in its entirety in cassette 1. the second specimen is submitted in a container of formalin labeled polyp transverse colon x2. it consists of tannish tissue measuring 6 x 6mm. the specimen is submitted in its entirety in cassette 2. microscopic examination performed.

subject: PATIENT
heading: PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSTS__page_no__3
text: pathologic diagnosts 1. ulceration with inflammation with changes suggestive of but not entirely diagnostic of ischemic colitis, specimen submitted as biopsy of ascending colon (see note). 2. tubular adenoma(s), specimen submitted as tissue from transverse colon. note: the ascending colon specimen shows changes which are somewhat suggestive of but not entirely diagnostic of ischemic colitis however the possibility of infectious colitis is difficult to exclude with certainty on histologic grounds alone. clinical correlation is suggested. electronically signed by [provider_name] [encounter_date] 1017 end of report [encounter_date] 03:25 pm [patient_name] dob [dob] 3/5
